transcript.clean_text: Mount Buller is the destination for the riders on stage four of the Jayco herald-sun tour at the end of a one hundred and six kilometers stage that is sure to test joy Hindley and the riders at the top of the general classification the riders will take on the climb of toll my after 44 kilometers before heading down the Mount Buller Road towards the final climb which is the best part of 17 kilometres long it's an average of 6% with the steepest section of hell corner towards the top rearing up over 13% some more familiar names in the early breakaway as we set off this morning and after some terrible cramps topped him short yesterday Beldon Hill was back on the offensive at the first intermediate sprinted the day after 21 kilometers provisionally reclaiming his sprints Jersey from Alberto die Nazy who's second place yesterday saw him take over the green jersey team some Webb were assembled on mass at the front of the peloton to keep their yellow jersey Hindley in good shape up until the final climb also in the day's eight-man move were yonas rush of EF Pro Cycling Ben Perry of Israel start-up nation david williams of cord amantha michael rice of ära pro racing connor Mirta of Oliver's real food racing ethan bat of black smoke and Ben Hills teammate at bridge lane Aiden to vie with ratchet two minutes and 32 seconds on GC the bunch were vigilant to keep the time gap manageable never more than about three minutes and 30 seconds has been hill claimed another maximum at the second intermediate sprint with 29 kilometers to go as we hit the foot of Mount Buller we soon saw riders starting to get dropped both from the peloton and the breakaway now only three riders out front in the form of Rush Perry and 2v it was always going to be a tough ask for them to stay away from the motivated group of favourites behind and when we got hold of live pictures again with 2.3 kilometers ago we saw fresh attacks coming from behind James piccoli of Israel start-up nation was up the road then hot pursuit was simon yates he had his teammate dion smith positioned as a go-between with rush still in the mix after the rest of his breakaway companions had drifted back piccoli was on the final switchbacks with the kilometre to go some terribly steep ramps to contend with for the time two young Canadian but behind white jersey set Berwick saw the opportunity in the gradients launching is attacked from third wheel the move that Henry had to cover with the st. George Wright are only six seconds behind him on GC Yates was quickly overtaken as the two protagonists followed by Jesse u ative teams of horror drove their way up the hill Berwick was applying as much pressure as he could muster as they caught piccoli with 500 metres to go the stage win as well as the overall now on the line the pair were locked together until the last 150 metres also where Hindley made his move nudging up on the inside and getting ahead as we came in towards the barriers there was not a whole lot to separate them by the top but the yellow jersey henley made it stick and cross the line first to crown a momentous week in his young career this is your top ten with J vine and u''t climbing well to get ahead of a clutch of World Tour riders including former overall winner Damien housen who wasn't able to follow the big move when it went a lot of pressure on this man at the start of the day but he shouldered it with style clearly the strongest rider on the climb to a to big mountains conquered this week his advantage is up to ten seconds on Berwick who continues to impress only 20 years of age Houston is down to third ahead of Neilson powerless while James or and climbs into the top ten after a bad day on the climb for Jimmy Whelan with just the critérium in Melbourne to conclude the race tomorrow it's looking likely that Jay Henley will win this title but it's never over till it's over see you tomorrow for stage 5
